name: Drunken Fish
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Fish, Whole (Red Snapper or similar)
2 each Ancho Chiles, Dried OR 1/2 tsp Red Peppers, Crushed
2 tbsp Olive Oil or Vegetable Oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Use fresh or frozen whole dressed Red Snapper or other whole fish.
2. Peel, seed, and chop the medium tomatoes.
3. Slice the pimentos stuffed olives. If using capers, prepare them as desired.
4. Thaw the fish if it is frozen.
5. Cut the ancho chiles open, discard stems and seeds, then chop into small pieces with a sharp knife or scissors.
6. Place the chopped chiles in a bowl and cover with boiling water. Let steep for 45 to 60 minutes, then drain.
7. Blend the drained chiles with red wine until nearly smooth.
8. In a medium saucepan, cook onion and garlic in hot oil until tender but not brown.
9. Add the blended chile-wine mixture (or crushed red peppers and wine), tomatoes, water, parsley, sugar, salt, oregano, and cumin to the saucepan.
10. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
11. Place the fish in a greased baking dish. Season the cavity with salt and pepper.
12. Stir in the sliced olives and capers (if using), then pour over the fish.
13. Cover and bake at 350°F until the fish flakes easily when tested with a fork, about 45 to 60 minutes.
14. Carefully remove the fish to a serving platter. Serve with the remaining sauce.
